_comment: Single source of truth for the cross-channel parity tests (bd empyrean-na4h.2, ADR cross-channel-parity). Consumed by: empyrean-core/src/bin/parity-oracle.rs (generates core_parity_oracle.json from `scenarios`), empyrean-py/tests/test_core_parity.py, empyrean/tests/parity.rs (wrapper), and the cli parity test. Each channel runs `scenarios` through its own API, projects to the fingerprint vocabulary, and diffs against the committed oracle using `known_drops` + `tolerances`. Add a scenario / drop ONCE here and every channel picks it up.

tolerances._comment: rtol/atol are the default; a scenario may override rtol (see impactor). The oracle (empyrean-core) and the channels (libempyrean dylib) are SEPARATELY compiled, so a chaotic trajectory amplifies ULP-level codegen differences — fine for well-conditioned cases (apophis, 1e-6) but not the near-surface impactor, whose covariance-derived fields (sigma_distance) drift a few e-6. The impactor's job is drop detection (0.0-vs-real signals, caught at any tolerance), so it earns a loose rtol.

scenarios.name: apophis
scenarios._comment: Apophis at MJD 61000 TDB (SBDB). Fires close approaches / periapses / possible impacts.

scenarios.name: impactor
scenarios._comment: 2008 TC3 final approach (MJD 54746 TDB), cometary->cartesian of the SBDB elements (villeneuve test_jet2.rs). Fires impacts / atmospheric + shadow entries.

_known_drops_comment: All C-ABI event-field drops (empyrean-14cz.1 periapsis relative state, .2 possible-impact payload, .3 shadow geometry) are now wired through to the wrapper + py channels — known_drops is empty. The CLI keeps its own channel-specific allow-list (CLI_EXTRA_DROPS in empyrean-cli/tests/parity.rs) because its events FILE writer is still common-fields-only (empyrean-i7u5 events half).
